introduction: The Modern Coding Theory Lab offers in-depth virtual experiments on encoding and decoding forward-error correcting codes used in modern communication systems. It focuses on combating channel noise to achieve high-rate, high-fidelity communications. Students explore advanced coding techniques, gaining hands-on experience with real-world applications and practical problem-solving.

experiments.name: Maximum Likelihood (ML) Decoding of Codes on Binary Input Channels
Message Passing Decoding of LDPC Codes on Erasure Channel
Message Passing Decoding of LDPC Codes on Other Channels
Reed-Muller Codes: A Polynomial View of Encoding
Reed-Muller Codes: Generator and Parity Check Matrices, Minimum Distance
Majority Logic Decoding of Reed-Muller Codes
ML Decoding of First-Order Reed-Muller Codes

objective:
Mastering Error Correction: Decode binary linear codes over bit erasure, bit-flip, and Gaussian noise channels, enhancing error resilience.
Exploring LDPC & RM Codes:Analyze the structure, performance, and real-world applications of Low-Density Parity-Check and Reed-Muller codes.
Bridging Theory & Practice: Apply coding techniques beyond the classroom, gaining skills for self-implementation in various communication systems.

courseAlignment.description: The syllabi of this lab aligns to the following universities in India.
universities: Information Theory and Coding is a professional elective course in Electronics & Communication Engineering (PEC-ECEL03 and PEC-ECEL20) recommended in the AICTE model curriculum.
